channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: NPR went looking for Polymarket's Panama headquarters. It's elusive
item.description: The hugely popular prediction market was shut down by U.S. regulators in 2022 and re-opened in Panama, where it has benefited from tax and legal benefits for years.
item.content:encoded:
The hugely popular prediction market was shut down by U.S. regulators in 2022 and re-opened in Panama, where it has benefited from tax and legal benefits for years.

(Image credit: Eliana Morales Gil)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: The Iran war sent jet fuel prices sky-high. Here's what air travelers should know
item.description: Fuel is airlines' second-largest expense, and getting even costlier due to the U.S. war with Iran. Airlines are looking for ways to cope, starting by hiking checked baggage fees for most fliers.
item.content:encoded:
Fuel is airlines' second-largest expense, and getting even costlier due to the U.S. war with Iran. Airlines are looking for ways to cope, starting by hiking checked baggage fees for most fliers.

(Image credit: Kena Betancur)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: If you are struggling to make ends meet, NPR wants to hear from you
item.description: With gas prices and other necessities at record highs, families are struggling with costs. NPR wants to know how you're coping.
item.content:encoded:
With gas prices and other necessities at record highs, families are struggling with costs. NPR wants to know how you're coping.

(Image credit: Jenny Kane)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: GameStop makes unsolicited bid to acquire online auction giant eBay for $56B
item.description: Video game retailer GameStop wants to buy the considerably larger eBay for $56 billion. NPR's Leila Fadel asks Wall Street Journal reporter Lauren Thomas about the offer.
item.content:encoded: Video game retailer GameStop wants to buy the considerably larger eBay for $56 billion. NPR's Leila Fadel asks Wall Street Journal reporter Lauren Thomas about the offer.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Hotels have a big World Cup problem: Bookings are running far below projections
item.description: Nearly 80% of hotels surveyed by a body representing the industry reported bookings were running below projections — a worrisome sign with only weeks to go before the World Cup.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 80% of hotels surveyed by a body representing the industry reported bookings were running below projections — a worrisome sign with only weeks to go before the World Cup.

(Image credit: Jamie Squire)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: Spirit Airlines shutters after federal bailout falls through
item.description: Spirit Airlines announced over the weekend it would stop flying following years of financial struggles. The ultra-low-cost carrier wanted a $500 million federal bailout from the Trump administration.
item.content:encoded: Spirit Airlines announced over the weekend it would stop flying following years of financial struggles. The ultra-low-cost carrier wanted a $500 million federal bailout from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Gas prices went up more than 30 cents a gallon last week. How high could they go?
item.description: U.S. gas prices were nearly $3 an average prior to the start of the war in Iran.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. gas prices were nearly $3 an average prior to the start of the war in Iran.

(Image credit: Jenny Kane)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: He recorded his quest for tariff refunds. It shows why billions may never get repaid
item.description: After the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, Richard Brown began lining up the paperwork he needed to get his refund. Experts say many businesses may never get their money back.
item.content:encoded:
After the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, Richard Brown began lining up the paperwork he needed to get his refund. Experts say many businesses may never get their money back.

(Image credit: Daniel Lozada for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: How algorithms wreaked havoc with these workers' schedules and cut their pay
item.description: Hourly workers across a number of industries have long been grappling with unstable schedules and pay as their employers use software to slash labor costs and maximize productivity.
item.content:encoded:
Hourly workers across a number of industries have long been grappling with unstable schedules and pay as their employers use software to slash labor costs and maximize productivity.

(Image credit: Huo Jingnan)
item.dc:creator: Huo Jingnan

item.title: Oil and gas companies making hay by making plastic?
item.description: Author Beth Gardiner talks about her new book, 'Plastic Inc.' and about what she learned about the relationship between oil and gas companies and the plastics industry.
item.content:encoded: Author Beth Gardiner talks about her new book, 'Plastic Inc.' and about what she learned about the relationship between oil and gas companies and the plastics industry.
item.dc:creator: Kai McNamee
